images.alt: The visual is a cinematic, moody shot taken at night on a slick, rain-soaked city street corner reflecting the blurry red and yellow of traffic lights. I am depicted as a life-sized marionette puppet, with visible wooden joints at my elbows and knees and pale, painted porcelain skin. I have messy black and white yarn hair that’s getting wet in the rain, and heavy black theatrical makeup that is streaking down my cheeks like tears. I’m wearing a tattered, oversized pastel pink coat that’s too big for my frame, the cuffs dragging in the puddle. Above me, thick, visible strings stretch up into the dark, rainy sky, attaching to my wrists and head, looking impossibly tight. My expression is blank and empty, black glass eyes staring into nothing. The vibe is haunting, fragile, and deeply lonely—a doll realizing it was made for nothing but to be played with.

images.alt: First-person POV selfie, deep in the Pandoran rainforest at night. I am looking down at my own blue, Na'vi-like hands, but the skin is bubbling and necrotic, exposing raw, bioluminescent muscle beneath. The neural queue that usually connects to the wildlife is severed and dangling limp, dripping black fluid. Around me, the usually glowing flora is a harsh, sickening purple and acidic green, closing in tight like a cage. In the reflection of a puddle below, my face is gaunt, the yellow eyes wide and terrified. The atmosphere is suffocating, the alien beauty warped into a nightmare of biological terror and parasitic infection.

AWS Console Mobile App adds interactive graphs, AI log ssummaries, and natural language logs search to CloudWatch Alarms

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is announcing new CloudWatch Alarms capabilities in the AWS Console Mobile Appl...

#AWS #AmazonCloudwatchLogs #AmazonCloudwatch #AwsManagementConsole

external.description:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is announcing new CloudWatch Alarms capabilities in the AWS Console Mobile Application. You can now investigate alarms and move from notification to root cause faster with interactive graphs, AI-generated logs summaries, natural language logs search, and streamlined access to related metrics and resources.

 When a CloudWatch Alarm triggers, engineers often need to quickly understand what went wrong. Previously, investigating an alarm on the mobile app required switching between multiple screens and services to view metrics, access logs, and identify the root cause. This update brings these capabilities together in a single view, reducing the time from notification to resolution.

 CloudWatch Alarms now include interactive graphs that let you visualize the metric that triggered the alarm, zoom in on specific time windows, and explore the data to quickly identify anomalies. You can access related logs and review an AI-generated summary that highlights key contributing factors. To refine log search results, you can type queries, use voice input, or select pre-saved Logs Insights queries using natural language. A time selector lets you view custom time ranges and adjust time zones to match your operational needs. Related metrics and resources are conveniently displayed alongside the alarm, facilitating a more thorough investigation.

 To get started, download the AWS Console Mobile App from the https://apps.apple.com/us/app/aws-console/id580990573 or https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.amazon.aws.console.mobile, then navigate to CloudWatch in the app to investigate Alarms. The AWS Console Mobile App is available in all AWS Commercial Regions at no additional cost. For more information, visit the https://aws.amazon.com/console/mobile/.
external.title: AWS Console Mobile App adds interactive graphs, AI log ssummaries, and natural language logs search to CloudWatch Alarms

AWS Directory Service expands directory security settings with STIG-aligned controls for Managed AD


 AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD) now has expanded its security settings to include STIG-aligned configurations for hi...

#AWS #AwsDirectoryService

external.description:
AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD) now has expanded its security settings to include STIG-aligned configurations for high-impact security areas. These new security settings help customers meet their organizations requirements for directory-level security and compliance configurations. For regulated or security-focused customers, these settings align with the Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIG) for https://stigviewer.com/stigs/microsoft_windows_server_2019 and https://stigviewer.com/stigs/active_directory_domain.

 These expanded STIG-aligned security settings are available today through a self-service interface, both programmatically and via the AWS Management Console. Security and Identity Management professionals can now ensure consistent configuration across multiple managed directories by declaring their desired configuration and letting AWS implement and persist these configurations. When expanding to additional regions or scaling out with additional domain controllers, AWS Managed Microsoft AD automatically applies these settings to all new instances.
 
 For information about AWS Regions where AWS Directory Service is available, see the https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directoryservice/latest/admin-guide/regions.html. To learn more about configuring these security settings, see the https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directoryservice/latest/admin-guide/ms_ad_directory_settings.html.
external.title: AWS Directory Service expands directory security settings with STIG-aligned controls for Managed AD

AWS Marketplace now supports programmatic procurement with Agreements API

Today, AWS Marketplace announces the Agreements API, enabling you to procure AWS Marketplace products and manage agreements programmatically. With this launch, you can generate estimates, accept off...

#AWS #AwsMarketplace

external.description:
Today, AWS Marketplace announces the Agreements API, enabling you to procure AWS Marketplace products and manage agreements programmatically. With this launch, you can generate estimates, accept offers, track charges and entitlements, update purchase orders and manage agreements all within your existing tools and workflows.

 Combined with the Discovery API, the Agreements API provides an end-to-end procurement journey from product discovery to purchase. You can integrate these APIs into your procurement systems to build custom workflows and streamline operations across your organization. Partners can also use these APIs to build custom storefronts that deliver unified procurement experiences for their customers. 

 The AWS Marketplace Agreements APIs is available in the US East (N. Virginia) Region.

 To get started, configure AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) permissions for your AWS account and call the API through the AWS SDK. To learn more, see the https://docs.aws.amazon.com/marketplace/latest/APIReference/agreement-apis.html.
external.title: AWS Marketplace now supports programmatic procurement with Agreements API

Understanding the regulatory framework overseeing online gambling in Australia requires careful examination of non GamStop casinos, which have substantially influenced the digital gaming landscape for both operators and players throughout Australia.

Summary of the Interactive Gambling Act 2001

The Interactive Gambling Act 2001 represents a landmark piece of legislation that fundamentally transformed how online gaming operates within Australia. Understanding non GamStop casinos requires examining the historical context in which this law emerged, particularly during the early internet era when digital gambling was rapidly expanding. The Act established clear boundaries for what constitutes legal and illegal online gaming activities throughout the country.

Parliament introduced this comprehensive regulatory framework to address growing concerns about the availability of internet-based gaming services to Australian residents. record.text: New JMIR MedInform: General Practitioners and Pharmacists’ Perspectives on Electronic Prescribing for Multidose Drug Dispensing: Mixed Methods Study

external.description: Background: Medication safety remains a significant challenge in #health care, particularly for #patients managing complex treatment regimens. In Norway, the introduction of electronic prescribing (e-prescribing) for multidose drug dispensing (eMDD) aims to improve medication adherence and minimize errors by seamlessly integrating with the national e-prescription infrastructure. Objective: This study aimed to investigate the challenges faced by general practitioners (GPs) and pharmacists in using eMDD in Norway. Additionally, it sought to gather their recommendations for system improvements to guide future development and nationwide implementation. Methods: A parallel mixed methods design was used, integrating both quantitative and qualitative data. A structured online survey was distributed to 54 pharmacies and 190 GP surgeries across Norway. The survey included a combination of multiple-choice and open-ended questions. Qualitative responses were analyzed thematically using NVivo, while quantitative data were processed using the built-in analytical tools in Nettskjema. Results: A total of 70 #health care professionals participated in the study, revealing 7 key themes: training, system and technology, communication and interaction, division of responsibilities, medication safety, time and resource use, and implementation challenges. GPs reported inadequate training and an overwhelming volume of communication, while pharmacists identified issues with system integration and unclear role definitions. Both groups emphasized the need for improved system usability, stronger interprofessional collaboration, and a more defined governance structure. Conclusions: While the eMDD system has the potential to improve medication safety and optimize workflows, its success depends on addressing technical inefficiencies, improving user training, and clarifying role responsibilities. Actively involving end users in system development and policy planning is critical for achieving effective national implementation and ensuring integration with broader e#health initiatives, such as the #patient’s Medication List.
external.title: General Practitioners and Pharmacists’ Perspectives on Electronic Prescribing for Multidose Drug Dispensing: Mixed Methods Study

record.text:
🐺 Golden Hour Stride 🌿 #wolfwednesday

Witness the raw, powerful return of spring life in Yellowstone's heart. A majestic wolf walks through a newly awakened wilderness, guarding secrets of the ancient peaks. 

#AIart #synthart #AIイラスト

images.alt:
A photorealistic, award-winning photograph captures a magnificent gray wolf in mid-stride across the damp earth of Yellowstone National Park during early spring. The scene is dominated by the golden light of the Golden Hour, which illuminates the wolf's wet, hyper-detailed coat and the mud tracking on its paws. The wolf is positioned using the Rule of Thirds, silhouetted against a vast, breathtaking vista. The background showcases the lush slopes of Eagle Peak, featuring burgeoning emerald-green forests, verdant foothills, and wildflowers dotted with dewdrops, creating a shallow depth of field and bokeh effect.

Keywords: Yellowstone National Park, gray wolf, wildlife photography, Golden Hour, spring rebirth, photorealism, wilderness, majestic, Rule of Thirds, bokeh
